architecture.summary: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) trained on Qwen3 instruction-tuned language models to decompose superposed activations into sparse, interpretable features. SAEs are trained layer-wise at three activation sites (residual streams, MLP outputs, attention outputs) across model sizes 1.7B–8B. The system learns a reconstruction mapping from model activations to sparse feature codes and back, enabling feature-level analysis and causal steering interventions via activation manipulation.

biblio.title: Discovering Millions of Interpretable Features with Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: Qwen3-Instruct SAE covers layer-wise SAEs trained on Qwen3-1.7B, Qwen3-4B, and Qwen3-8B at three key activation sites: residual streams, MLP outputs, and attention outputs
Systematic evaluation reveals distinct sparsity–fidelity trade-offs across layers and components using activation-level reconstruction metrics and model-level recovery metrics
Selected SAE features can causally steer instruction-tuned Qwen3 models toward refusal behavior in refusal-steering case study
SAEs decompose superposed language model representations into sparse and interpretable features
